title: ReusStdFlow: A Standardized Reusability Framework for Dynamic Workflow
abstract: To address the “reusability dilemma” and struc- tural hallucinations in enterprise Agentic AI, this paper proposes ReusStdFlow, a frame- work centered on a novel “Extraction-Storage- Construction” paradigm. The framework decon- structs heterogeneous, platform-specific Domain- Specific Languages (DSLs) into standardized, modular workflow segments. It employs a dual- knowledge architecture—integrating graph and vector databases—to facilitate synergistic retrieval of both topological structures and functional se- mantics. Finally, workflows are intelligently as- sembled using a retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) strategy. Tested on 200 real-world n8n workflows, the system achieves over 90% accuracy in both extraction and construction. This frame- work provides a standardized solution for the au- tomated reorganization and efficient reuse of enter- prise digital assets.
summary:
This research paper proposes a framework called ReusStdFlow to address issues with reusing and structuring artificial intelligence (AI) workflows in enterprises. The framework:

1. Breaks down complex workflows into standardized, modular segments.
2. Uses a dual-knowledge architecture to store and retrieve both structural and functional information.
3. Assembles workflows intelligently using a retrieval-augmented generation strategy.

Testing on 200 real-world workflows showed that the system achieved over 90% accuracy in both extracting and constructing workflows. The ReusStdFlow framework provides a standardized solution for efficiently reusing and reorganizing digital assets in enterprises, addressing the "reusability dilemma" and reducing structural hallucinations in AI systems.
